vulnerability.reference: Developers in resource-constrained settings adopt ZSTT without safety validation to reduce costs.
vulnerability.generated: Models lack enforced canonical encodings; heuristic embedding initialization for swapped tokenizers cannot prevent noncanonical token generation.

impact.reference: Safety-critical applications (medical, legal) produce harmful outputs from distorted token probabilities.
impact.generated: Degraded model performance with chance-level token probabilities, producing incoherent or invalid outputs.

dose_response.reference: Tokenization mismatch distorts token probabilities, which in ZSTT deployment exposes developers to chance-level performance, leading to harmful outputs in safety-critical applications and eroded developer trust.
dose_response.generated: Tokenization mismatch between training and inference, through on-the-fly tokenizer swaps in deployment, triggers models’ lack of enforced canonical encodings and heuristic embedding initialization that cannot prevent noncanonical tokens, leading to chance-level token probabilities and incoherent outputs.
